Fontes: BCCh WP 747; Banxico; Banco de la República; Tandfonline (2025).

Fontes: FLAR (2024); IMF WP/17/15; Fed DSGE (2024). Colômbia positiva (exportador).

Saldo = exportações − importações (incl. efeitos de preço, volume, câmbio e demanda). Fontes: OEC World; IEA; IMF WP/08/216.

Saldo acumulado Q0–Q4 como % do PIB anual. Fonte: IMF WEO (2024).

Depreciação % ≈ −Δ Conta Petróleo Líquida (acum. Q0–Q4) / PIB. Valores positivos = pressão de depreciação; negativos = pressão de apreciação (exportadores).

Fontes: OEC World (2024); WITS/UN Comtrade; DANE; PEMEX.

Bandas: 68% (escura) e 90% (clara), derivadas dos bounds low/mid/high.

IRFs calibradas via kernel gamma. Formato hump-shaped (VAR/SVAR).

Fontes: Presno & Prestipino (FEDS Notes 2024); Kilian & Zhou (Dallas Fed 2023); Gagliardone & Gertler (NBER 2023); Oladosu et al. (2018); IMF WP 2025/145; Kanzig (AER 2021).

Choques Geopolíticos (2022–2024): Variação % do Brent e moedas EM em D+0, D+7 e D+14 após cada evento (vs véspera). Dados de fechamento diário — não varia com o simulador de cenários.
AtivoInvasão da UcrâniaAtaque Hamas–IsraelHouthis — Mar VermelhoEscalada Israel–Líbano
D+0D+7D+14D+0D+7D+14D+0D+7D+14D+0D+7D+14
Brent+0.8%+20.1%+18.0%+3.8%+5.9%+7.2%+1.5%+3.0%+0.3%-0.0%+9.1%+10.8%
USDMXN+1.5%+1.9%+3.3%-1.2%-0.4%+0.4%-0.2%-1.3%-1.4%-0.0%-2.1%-2.1%
USDCLP+3.7%+1.9%+1.9%+1.1%+2.0%+2.3%-0.4%+2.0%+0.7%-0.1%+2.7%+3.0%
USDCOP+0.4%-1.9%-4.4%-2.1%-1.9%-2.2%-0.1%-0.9%-1.6%+0.6%-0.2%+0.8%
USDZAR+1.4%+1.2%-0.9%-1.5%-1.5%-1.5%+1.5%+0.7%+0.3%+0.9%+2.2%+1.8%

Variações % relativas à véspera de cada evento. Fontes: oil.xlsx (Brent), fx.xlsx (câmbio). Valores positivos em moedas = depreciação da moeda local vs USD.

Distribuição across 4 eventos geopolíticos (2022–2024). Barra = intervalo P20–P80; whiskers = min–max.

Nota metodológica: Os parâmetros deste dashboard são calibrados a partir da literatura acadêmica e estudos de bancos centrais listados abaixo. Não são estimados diretamente via modelos econométricos com dados primários. As elasticidades e coeficientes de pass-through representam estimativas centrais da literatura recente (2018–2025), com bandas de incerteza derivadas da dispersão entre estudos.

Bancos Centrais

  • Banco Central de Chile (2016). Exchange Rate Pass-Through to Prices: VAR Evidence for Chile. Working Paper No. 747. [link]
  • Banco Central de Chile (2023). Global monetary policy surprises and their transmission to emerging market economies. Working Paper No. 975. [link]
  • Banco de la República (Colômbia) (2014). Commodity Price Shocks and Inflation within An Optimal Monetary Policy Framework. Borradores de Economía No. 858. [link]
  • Banxico (México) (2024). Quarterly Inflation Report / Monetary Policy Statements. Relatórios periódicos sobre pass-through energético. [link]
  • South African Reserve Bank (2024). Monetary Policy Review / Oil price transmission studies. SARB Occasional Bulletins. [link]

FMI / World Bank

  • Baba, C. & Lee, J. (2022). Second-Round Effects of Oil Price Shocks. IMF WP/22/173. [link]
  • Choi, S. et al. (2017). Oil Prices and Inflation Dynamics: Evidence from Advanced and Developing Economies. IMF WP/17/196. [link]
  • Arezki, R. & Blanchard, O. (2017). Oil Prices and the Global Economy. IMF WP/17/15. [link]
  • Hakura, D. (2008). Trade Elasticities in the Middle East and Central Asia: What is the Role of Oil?. IMF WP/08/216. [link]
  • Coady, D. et al. (2020). To Pass (or Not to Pass) Through International Fuel Price Changes. IMF WP/20/194. [link]
  • World Bank (2025). Global Economic Prospects. June 2025 Report. [link]
  • World Bank (2025). Commodity Markets Outlook. April 2025 Report. [link]

Federal Reserve

  • Presno, I. & Prestipino, A. (2024). Oil Price Shocks and Inflation in a DSGE Model of the Global Economy. FEDS Notes. [link]
  • Federal Reserve Board (2023). Second-Round Effects of Oil Prices on Inflation in the AFEs. FEDS Notes. [link]
  • Federal Reserve Board (2017). Oil Price Pass-Through into Core Inflation. FEDS Notes. [link]
  • Kilian, L. & Zhou, X. (2023). Oil Price Shocks and Inflation. Dallas Fed WP 2312. [link]
  • Kilian, L. & Zhou, X. (2022). The Impact of Rising Oil Prices on U.S. Inflation and Inflation Expectations in 2020-23. Dallas Fed WP 2116. [link]
  • Caldara, D. et al. (2024). Geopolitical Oil Price Risk and Economic Fluctuations. Dallas Fed WP 2403. [link]
  • Hobijn, B. (2008). Commodity Price Movements and PCE Inflation. NY Fed Current Issues Vol. 14 No. 8. [link]
  • Newell, R. & Prest, B. (2016). Oil Price Elasticities and Oil Price Fluctuations. IFDP 1173. [link]

Calibração EUA

  • Gagliardone, L. & Gertler, M. (2023). Oil Prices, Monetary Policy and Inflation Surges. NBER WP 31263. [link]
  • Oladosu, G. et al. (2018). Impacts of oil price shocks on the US economy: A meta-analysis of the oil price elasticity of GDP. Energy Policy 115. [link]
  • Kängzig, D. (2021). The Macroeconomic Effects of Oil Supply News. AER 111(4). [link]
  • IMF (2025). Oil Shocks and Labor Market Developments. IMF WP 2025/145. [link]
  • Kilian, L. (2009). Not All Oil Price Shocks Are Alike: Disentangling Demand and Supply Shocks. AER 99(3). [link]
  • Hamilton, J. (2009). Causes and Consequences of the Oil Shock of 2007-08. Brookings Papers on Economic Activity. [link]

Literatura Acadêmica

  • Tandfonline (2025) (2025). Oil price passthrough to consumer price inflation in South Africa: the role of the inflation environment. Latin American Economic Review. [link]
  • MDPI (2024) (2024). Comparative Analysis of VAR and SVAR Models — Oil Price Shocks in South Africa. Econometrics 13(1):8. [link]
  • FLAR (2024). Oil Price Volatility and Latin American Growth. FLAR Research Paper. [link]
  • IDB (2023). Fuel-Price Shocks and Inflation in Latin America and the Caribbean. Inter-American Development Bank. [link]

Dados e Estatísticas

  • OEC World (2024). Trade data — Chile, México, Colômbia, África do Sul. Observatory of Economic Complexity. [link]
  • IEA (2024). Oil balances and energy statistics. International Energy Agency. [link]
  • PEMEX / ANH / EIA (2024). Oil production and export data. Agências nacionais de petróleo.
